title: Add remaining bronze/silver data quality checks
description: Extend data_quality_checks.Notebook with the 3 checks not covered by task-007's 9 functions: bronze date-range validation, silver data-type consistency, and silver completeness. Identified by the 2026-05-24 spec reconciliation of section Data Quality Checks Needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Bronze: date_range_validation check added — flags future dates and implausible/out-of-range dates on date fields (e.g. dbo.Procurement.Date); informational, no false positives on synthetic data
Silver: data_type_consistency check added — validates column types conform to the expected schema after the bronze-to-silver casts
Silver: completeness check added — OR documented in-notebook that bronze required_field_completeness already covers it (implementer decides and records the rationale)

Each new check follows the established task-007 pattern: returns a metric, persists to gold_quality_history (refresh_timestamp, layer, entity, metric_name, metric_value, threshold, breach_flag), graceful try/except, wired into the notebook's check sequence
Spec section Data Quality Checks Needed boxes flipped to [x] for whichever checks are implemented (via /iterate, per DEC-016)
notes: The 3 unchecked items remaining in spec section Data Quality & Validation after the task-007 audit on 2026-05-24 (9 of 12 framework checks were already implemented).
Portfolio-scale polish, not blocking core work (006_3, 010, 012 take priority).

Completeness nuance: bronze required_field_completeness already validates required-field nulls before silver; add a silver completeness check only if post-join completeness matters — otherwise document that bronze covers it rather than adding a redundant check.
Spec checkbox updates must route through /iterate (DEC-016), not a direct edit.
CLAUDE: Add the check functions to data_quality_checks.Notebook following the task-007 pattern (Claude can do this autonomously).
ERIK: Deploy the updated notebook to Fabric and run it to confirm the new checks pass on real data (runtime validation is not_applicable locally — Fabric needs a PySpark environment).

Added 3 checks mirroring task-007 pattern (spark.table() read, graceful try/except that never false-positives on missing tables, log_check_result -> gold_quality_history with the 7-column schema, registered in CHECK_TO_DIMENSION): (1) Bronze date_range_validation — informational (pass/warning, never fail), flags future and implausibly-old dates on bronze_procurement_transactional.Date via a [2015-01-01, today] window (today resolves at runtime in Fabric); zero false positives on ~2024 synthetic data. (2) Silver data_type_consistency — validates silver_procurement column types after bronze->silver casts (date/string/decimal), case-insensitive name + substring type match (PySpark-version tolerant); distinct from bronze schema_validation which runs pre-cast. (3) Silver completeness — CHOSE TO ADD (not document bronze as covering it): silver_procurement is a LEFT JOIN onto bronze_supplier_ref on SupplierName, so an unmatched supplier leaves headquarterscountry/productioncountry NULL on an otherwise-valid row — a post-join risk bronze required_field_completeness (pre-join) structurally cannot see; in-notebook RATIONALE comment added.

Pure predicate logic extracted to src/transformations/data_quality.py (mirrored inline in the notebook since Fabric notebooks cannot import src/); 15 new unit tests added to tests/test_data_quality.py; full suite 62/62 pass, 0 regressions. Renumbered check banners to a global 1-12 sequence (Bronze 1-5, Silver 6-10, Gold 11-12). Notebook format verified: 17 CELL / 8 MARKDOWN / 18 METADATA markers balanced, 17/17 code cells parse.

FOLLOW-UP (orchestrator/user): flip spec section Data Quality Checks Needed boxes [ ]->[x] for the 3 checks via /iterate (DEC-016); optionally update the status note from '9 of these are implemented' -> '12 of these are implemented'.

DECISION (not formalized as DEC record — implementation-level, documented in-notebook per decisions.md 'Skip Records For'): chose to ADD a silver completeness check rather than declare bronze sufficient, because the silver left-join introduces post-join null risk bronze cannot detect. Options considered: (a) add silver check [selected], (b) document bronze covers it.

--- VERIFIED 2026-05-24 (verify-agent, PASS attempt 1) --- 7 checks pass (runtime_validation partial: 62/62 unit tests green locally, live Fabric run deferred to deployment per HANDOFF). Minor doc nit found and fixed post-verification by orchestrator: section banner check-counts were stale after renumber (Bronze/Silver), corrected to actual counts. user_review_pending set — Erik's Fabric deploy+run tracked via test_protocol.

issues.description: Notebook section banner check-counts were stale after the global renumber (e.g. 'Bronze Layer Checks (4 checks)' with 5 present). Fixed post-verification by orchestrator. No functional impact.

task_verification.notes: All 3 intended DQ checks implemented correctly: Bronze date_range_validation (informational, null-orthogonal, [2015-01-01, today] window), Silver data_type_consistency (post-cast, distinct from bronze pre-cast schema_validation), Silver completeness (post-join LEFT-JOIN null risk, sound in-notebook rationale). All follow task-007's pattern (graceful try/except, log_check_result -> gold_quality_history 7-col schema, registered in CHECK_TO_DIMENSION + check sequence). 15 new unit tests pass; full suite 62/62, 0 regressions. Notebook format intact (17 CELL/8 MARKDOWN/18 METADATA, valid Python). Scope clean (3 declared files match diff; spec checkboxes correctly left for /iterate per DEC-016). Runtime: unit tests validate predicate logic locally; live Fabric Delta-table execution deferred to Erik's deployment (same not_applicable-locally constraint as task-007).

issues.description: Stale section-banner check-counts after renumber; fixed post-verification.

verification_history.notes: PASS attempt 1. 3 checks correct + pattern-faithful; 62/62 tests; notebook format intact; live Fabric run deferred to deployment (test_protocol written).

test_protocol.summary: Deploy the updated data_quality_checks notebook to Fabric and run it to confirm the 3 new checks execute against live bronze/silver Delta tables (local PySpark cannot reach the lakehouse).

steps.instruction: Sync the updated notebook to the Fabric workspace and open data_quality_checks.Notebook
steps.expected: Notebook loads; Bronze section shows the Date Range Validation check, Silver shows Data Type Consistency and Completeness (Post-Join)

steps.instruction: Run all cells against the live lakehouse (oem_lh)
steps.expected: Bronze date-range prints [PASS] (0 out-of-range dates on ~2024 synthetic data); Silver type-consistency prints [PASS] (all silver_procurement columns conform); Silver completeness prints [PASS] or a small post-join incomplete count if any supplier_ref join misses exist. No [SKIP] for these tables.

steps.instruction: Query gold_quality_history for this run's new metrics
steps.expected: 3 new rows present with the 7-column schema (refresh_timestamp, layer, entity, metric_name, metric_value, threshold, breach_flag)
steps.command: SELECT metric_name, metric_value, breach_flag FROM oem_lh.gold_quality_history WHERE metric_name IN ('dq_date_range_validation','dq_data_type_consistency','dq_silver_completeness') ORDER BY refresh_timestamp DESC

test_protocol.automated_results: Local runtime validation passed: 15 new unit tests + 62/62 full suite green (predicate logic confirmed). Remaining: notebook execution against live Fabric Delta tables (cannot run locally).
